combat_history.event: HMAS Adelaide and Canberra conducted evacuation of civilians from bushfire-affected areas, demonstrating humanitarian capability. Adelaide evacuated over 1,100 people from Mallacoota beach.
combat_history.significance: First major operational use, proved value for domestic emergency response and validated well-deck operations in challenging conditions

combat_history.event: HMAS Adelaide deployed on 4-month regional engagement with 13 nations, operating MRH-90 and Chinook helicopters, conducting amphibious exercises.
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated sustained regional presence capability and interoperability with key Indo-Pacific partners

combat_history.event: Both ships participated in large-scale US-Australia amphibious exercise, with HMAS Canberra operating F-35B aircraft from US Marines for first time.
combat_history.significance: Proved flight deck can handle F-35B despite lacking ski-jump, opening future capability options

modernization.description: Assessment of modifications needed to operate F-35B Lightning II fighters. Study concluded ships can operate F-35B with heat-resistant deck coating and minor modifications.
modernization.impact: Opens potential for genuine aircraft carrier capability, significantly enhancing power projection

modernization.description: Upgrade to satellite communications and data links to support Joint All-Domain Command and Control (JADC2) concepts with US forces.
modernization.impact: Better integration with US Navy and enhanced command and control for distributed operations

vulnerabilities.description: Extremely limited air defense capability with only CIWS and small-caliber guns. No medium or long-range SAM systems. Completely dependent on escort vessels or friendly air cover.
vulnerabilities.context: Critical weakness in contested environments where adversaries have significant missile and air capabilities
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Always operates with air defense escorts (Hobart-class destroyers) when in threat environments

vulnerabilities.description: 21-knot maximum speed is slow by modern naval standards. Large signature makes it easy to detect and target. Minimal armor protection.
vulnerabilities.context: Vulnerable to modern anti-ship missiles and submarines, requires substantial defensive escort
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Relies on distance from threats and escort protection rather than organic survivability

vulnerabilities.description: Limited aviation fuel storage restricts sustained high-tempo flight operations, particularly with fuel-hungry aircraft like Chinook helicopters.
vulnerabilities.context: Limits operational endurance in extended operations far from resupply
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Requires regular replenishment from support vessels during extended operations

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Regional presence operations, humanitarian assistance, amphibious exercises with allies, disaster relief
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Usually operates with 1-2 Hobart-class destroyers, 1 ANZAC-class frigate, and Supply-class AOR
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High availability despite complexity. Both ships have maintained good operational tempo since commissioning.

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Izumo converting to F-35B carriers but smaller (19,500t vs 27,500t). Canberra has superior amphibious capability with well deck. Izumo has better self-defense systems.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Pacific helicopter carriers: comparing Australia's amphibious focus vs Japan's air defense conversion

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 075 is larger (40,000t) with stronger self-defense, carries more aircraft and troops. Canberra has superior sensors and electronics but much smaller capacity.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: David vs Goliath: How Australia's LHDs stack against China's amphibious fleet

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Near-identical hull but Juan Carlos retains ski-jump for Harrier operations. Spanish version has different radar and combat systems. Both share most capabilities.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Original vs copy: How Australia modified Spain's amphibious design

peer_comparisons.key_differences: QE is pure aircraft carrier (65,000t) with 36+ F-35B capability but no amphibious capacity. Completely different mission focus despite similar flight deck appearance.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Carrier vs amphibious ship: why Australia and Britain chose different paths

peer_comparisons.key_differences: America-class larger (45,000t) with no well deck but superior aviation facilities and self-defense. More optimized for aviation operations than amphibious assault.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Allied amphibious ships: US aviation focus vs Australian flexibility
